codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that fully covers all requirements. JSON data file contains all 4 trend directions with complete fields (name, heat, confidence, judgment state, audience fit, price-band fit, product-line breakdown for all 4 lines, risks, evidence, freshness). All 4 product lines present with correct opportunity/risk/movement status matching AGENTS.md verbatim. 7 evidence entries (exceeds 6+ requirement) with all required fields. 14-day change data with rising/cooling/newly-emerging arrays. CSS design system is comprehensive with custom properties for colors, typography, spacing, shadows, border-radius, and all 4 judgment state color mappings (green/blue/orange/gray). JS utilities provide well-structured data loading, component rendering, and navigation functions. All Chinese strings match AGENTS.md exactly. Code follows project conventions (vanilla JS, no frameworks, kebab-case CSS, camelCase JS).

issues.description: CSS variable --color-judgment-xuyaobuzhengjyu uses non-standard romanization for 需要补证据. Pinyin would be 'xuyaobuzhengju'. The 'jyu' suffix is unusual. This is internally consistent (same spelling in components.js badge classes), so it doesn't cause bugs, but could confuse future workers looking for the variable.

today_judgments includes 观察中:1 which is not in the feature description's specified values (值得跟:2, 短热噪音:1, 需要补证据:3). This is actually a reasonable addition since 多巴胺色系耳饰 has judgment_state=观察中, making the data internally consistent. The specified counts for other states match exactly.

The fetch path '/data/jade_dashboard.json' uses an absolute path from server root. This works correctly when served via 'python3 -m http.server 8765 --directory dashboard' as configured in services.yaml. However, if the serving configuration ever changes (e.g., serving from repo root), this path would break. A relative path like 'data/jade_dashboard.json' might be more portable, though current setup is fine.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not use agent-browser for visual verification, which the prototype-worker skill marks as 'REQUIRED for page features'. This feature is a data/foundation feature (not a page feature), so this is arguably not a deviation. However, the skill could be clearer about when agent-browser verification is required vs optional — currently the skill says 'REQUIRED for page features' under Manual verification but the worker correctly interpreted this as not applicable for a foundation/data feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows no agent-browser tool calls. Skill SKILL.md section '4. Verify Your Work' says 'Manual verification with agent-browser (REQUIRED for page features)'. Feature creates CSS/JS/JSON files but no HTML pages.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The romanization scheme for Chinese terms in CSS/JS identifiers is inconsistent. 需要补证据 is romanized as 'xuyaobuzhengjyu' (non-standard), while other terms use standard pinyin (zhidegeng for 值得跟, guanchazu for 观察中, duanrezaoyin for 短热噪音). AGENTS.md doesn't specify a romanization convention for CSS class names. Subsequent workers building pages will need to reference these exact class names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/css/style.css line 28: --color-judgment-xuyaobuzhengjyu, and dashboard/js/components.js: badgeClassMap uses 'badge-xuyaobuzhengjyu'. Standard pinyin would be 'xuyaobuzhengju'.

summary: Feature passes review. All validation assertions (VAL-DATA-001 through VAL-DATA-006) are satisfied. JSON data file is valid with 4 trend directions containing all required fields, 4 product lines with correct data, 7 evidence entries with freshness metadata, 14-day change data, and today's judgment counts. CSS design system defines comprehensive custom properties including judgment state colors (green/blue/orange/gray). JS utilities provide data loading, component rendering, and navigation functions. All Chinese strings match AGENTS.md verbatim. Two non-blocking issues noted: unusual romanization of 需要补证据 in CSS variable names, and absolute fetch path in data.js. Both are internally consistent and functional.
